actions.value:
Hi,

thanks for reaching out.

It's currently not possible within Edge.

 

All XRP wallets are required to hold at least 10 XRP at all times. This is an XRP policy called the Base Reserve (https://xrpl.org/reserves.html) and it applies to all XRP wallets. When you create a new XRP wallet in Edge, you'll see a message informing you about this.

 

The last 10 XRP is bound to your XRP account / public address on the blockchain, so these funds are not technically stuck in Edge.

We hope that XRP will change this policy in the near future, especially if the price keeps increasing!

 

If you want you can delete the XRP account by using an external app and an external service, but still you will need to burn 2 XRP and will recover 8, if you don't have any XRP token wallets such as Stably USD, Gatehub EUR, Bitstamp EUR...

In order to do so, you will need to export the master private key from your XRP wallet, download Xaman Wallet and import your XRP wallet there.

You can then use this website: https://xrpl.services/

Login using your Xaman and then follow the steps to delete your XRP account.

 

You can export your Master Private Key by going to the Assets screen. Then tap on the wallet you wish to view the key  / seeds for. Once in the wallet, tap on the three vertical dots (located upper right side of screen) and a new menu will pop up. Next tap View Master Private Key and enter your password and your key will be displayed.
 

WARNING: Exposing your private keys can be risky if you do not take the right precautions. Using a private browser or opening the derivation tool on an air-gapped PC will help prevent malicious software from stealing your keys. Most users will not need to follow these steps and they are presented for informational use only.

 

Please let us know if you have any questions.
